response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's resume does not align closely with the Principal Performance Architect role, which requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and performance analysis for deep-learning workloads. His background is primarily in software development, web/mobile applications, and general full-stack engineering, with no clear evidence of hardware-software co-design, SoC architecture, or AI accelerator optimization experience. His educational background (BS in Computer Science) and professional experience do not meet the required or preferred qualifications for this role.
strengths: Strong software engineering and full-stack development experience across multiple languages and frameworks
Proven ability to optimize performance in low-latency and high-throughput systems (e.g., micro-services handling millions of requests/second)
Open-source contributions and leadership in software craftsmanship practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD, refactoring)
concerns: No demonstrated experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator architectures
Lacks the required technical depth in deep-learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD architectures
Professional experience does not align with the specialized hardware/performance focus of the role (e.g., national instruments C/C++ work is not sufficient for AI accelerator optimization)
